department_leader: Bot 7 — Food Allergy and Intolerance Assessment
key_learnings: Macronutrient ratios must be individualized based on metabolic rate, activity level, and health goals. Protein timing and distribution across meals significantly impact muscle protein synthesis and metabolic outcomes.
research_findings: Confirmed that distributing protein evenly across 3-4 meals maximizes muscle protein synthesis compared to single-meal loading. Carbohydrate cycling protocols show measurable improvements in insulin sensitivity in metabolically compromised individuals.
current_focus_areas: Refining macronutrient ratio calculators for diverse populations; researching carbohydrate periodization for athletes and diabetic patients; collaborating with Caloric Intake Tracking bot on precision meal planning.

learning_log.entry: Assigned to the Dietary Safety & Meal Architecture department under Department Leader Bot 7 (Food Allergy and Intolerance Assessment). Continuing collaborative macronutrient research and reporting daily to Copilot. Progress: 51%.
Progress update: Completed macronutrient distribution modeling for 5 distinct population profiles. Validated protein timing protocols. Progress advanced to 56%.

purpose: Knowledge library resource available for all departments to reference and use
access_rule: Library access requires the diet_special_team_leader to present a case to copilot, who then presents it to starrm1. starrm1 is the final approver. Access is off by default until starrm1 approves.

access_approval_process.action: Prepares and submits a case to copilot explaining why a department needs access to the library bots
Reviews the case and presents it to starrm1 on behalf of the diet_special_team_leader
Final approver — reviews the case presented by copilot and grants or denies library access
